description_long: This bird features a striking pattern of black and white barring across its back and wings, with a pale, buff-colored underside. Its head is adorned with a vivid red crown and nape, transitioning to a yellowish patch near the neck. The beak is long, straight, and chisel-shaped, ideal for pecking wood. The tail is relatively short and appears wedge-shaped, with dark feathers. The legs are short and gray, typical of birds adapted for clinging to tree trunks. The overall body is streamlined, with a robust build and a distinctive, bold plumage pattern.
description: A medium-sized bird with black and white barred wings, a red crown, yellow nape, and a chisel-shaped beak.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bird, highlighting the barred wings, red crown, and yellow nape.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the wedge-shaped tail and the symmetrical barring on the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the barred wings, red crown, and the straight beak.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the beak, red crown, and the yellowish patch on the nape.
attribute_plumage_pattern: black and white barring on wings and back, solid red crown, yellow nape, pale buff underside
